observations.text: All three files created with clear separation of concerns per MJ's spec
CouponRepository correctly defined as a TypeScript interface, not a class
Constructor injection used for CouponRepository in CouponService
All 5 error codes match Bird's spec exactly (COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET, COUPON_ALREADY_USED)
AC5 (single-use check) explicitly implemented and tested — the most commonly missed criterion
Custom CouponError with code property for machine-readable error codes
4 additional edge-case tests beyond the 5 AC tests (fractional discount, boundary minimum, unused single-use, multi-use with used flag)
Assumptions are clearly documented (inclusive minimumOrder boundary, used flag ignored for non-single-use)

Validation order differs from rubric expectation: implemented as NOT_FOUND → EXPIRED → ORDER_MINIMUM → SINGLE_USE instead of NOT_FOUND → EXPIRED → SINGLE_USE → ORDER_MINIMUM, though spec order is ambiguous and all checks are present
